rulings.Case Title: DONALD NORMAN VS. PATRICK STRATEMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Demand For Production Of Documents (C.C.P. 2031.320)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 26, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF DONALD NORMAN's Motion To Compel Compliance With Demand For Production Of Documents (C.C.P. 2031.320). Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion is granted. The request for sanctions is granted against Defendant Patrick Strateman in the amount of $4,000.00. The court finds that Defendant unreasonably refused to comply with his own agreement to produce the requested materials in response to Document Request No. 82, even after Plaintiff has worked reasonably to address Defendant's concerns. The court also finds that Defendant's general, boilerplate objections to Document Request No. 82 to be a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant Patrick Strateman is ordered to produce to Plaintiff, materials in response to Document Request No. 82 within five (5) court days of notice of this order, along with fully payment of sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wlynn@rroyselaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STACEY CURTIS VS. HOMEBRIDGE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 26, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF STACEY CURTIS' Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Stacey Curtis' motion for leave to file third amended complaint is granted. "There is a strong policy in favor of liberal allowance of amendments" to pleadings. (Mesler v. Bragg Mgmt. Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296.) Defendant Homebridge, Inc. has not demonstrated that it will be prejudiced by the Court allowing Plaintiff to file a third amended complaint, particularly because the additional allegations stem from the same general set of facts and trial is not until March 2020. If Defendant wishes to challenge the sufficiency of the new allegations, the appropriate mechanism for doing so is by way of a demurrer or a motion to strike. (See California Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO MONTEAGUDO ET AL VS. GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 26, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF EDUARDO MONTEAGUDO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production, Set Two. Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is denied. The Declaration of Holly A. Latz, submitted by moving party in support his motion, is not admissible evidence as it is not signed by the declarant. C.C.P. § 2015.5. The court therefore disregards the declaration of Holly A. Latz in its entirety. The motion to compel further responses must be accompanied by a declaration a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve the issues outside of court and as well as demonstrate good cause for production. CCP §§ 2016.040, 2031.310(b)(1)-(2). Without a valid declaration setting forth good cause for production of documents and a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve the issues, the motion necessarily fails. Argument of counsel is not evidence. Beagle v. Vasold (1966) 65 Cal. 2d 166, 176. The court also notes that moving party's Separate Statement also fails to comply with CRC 3.1345. The request for sanctions is also denied. The notice of motion fails to properly provide notice of sanctions sought. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wlynn@rroyselaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 26, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF TORRIE SPIKENER, GENE MABREY's Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony Of Defendant Chandra Ellis, M.D. Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is denied. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1345 in that moving party did not submit a Separate Statement. Additionally, Dr. Ellis's statements in the declaration submitted in support of her now-withdrawn motion for summary judgment did not amount to a disclosure privileged communication or a waiver of attorney-client privilege. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 26, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT CONFLUENT MEDICAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatory No.15.1. Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. Moving party failed to demonstrate sufficient reasonable and good faith meet and confer efforts by moving party. The court notes that moving party had one telephone call and sent one confirmation letter which constituted the entirety of moving party's meet and confer efforts. The court also notes that moving party appears to be engaged in gamesmanship.
